model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Michigan State is a battle-tested 3-seed ranked 8th in the AP Poll with an SRS of 23.44 (10th nationally) and a schedule strength ranked 8th, while North Dakota State's SRS of just 2.50 reflects a 27-7 record built against the 280th-ranked schedule in the country. While NDSU's raw efficiency numbers are respectable (ORtg 56th, DRtg 42nd), those metrics are heavily inflated by Summit League competition, and the Bison's losses to mid-majors like UC Davis, UTEP, and St. Thomas (MN) expose vulnerabilities that Michigan State's Big Ten-caliber roster will exploit. Tom Izzo's legendary March track record combined with the Spartans' significant advantages in talent, experience, and strength of schedule make this a comfortable win for Michigan State despite NDSU's strong conference campaign.

reasoningStep.title: East 3/14: Michigan State vs North Dakota State
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan State's elite schedule strength, high SRS, and Tom Izzo's March pedigree give them a commanding edge over a North Dakota State team whose strong raw numbers were compiled against the 280th-ranked schedule nationally.
evidence: Michigan State: 25-7, SRS 23.44 (10th), SOS 12.91 (8th), ORtg 117.3 (49th), DRtg 101.7 (58th), AP #8
North Dakota State: 27-7, SRS 2.50 (131st), SOS -5.79 (280th), ORtg 116.1 (56th), DRtg 100.1 (42nd)
SRS gap of 20.94 points reflects enormous difference in competition level
NDSU lost to mid-major opponents (UC Davis, UTEP, St. Thomas MN) suggesting struggles against higher-level competition
Tom Izzo has reached 10 Final Fours and is one of the best March coaches in history

Now I have comprehensive data. Let me compile my analysis:

**Michigan State (3 seed):**
- Record: 25-7 (15-5 Big Ten, 2nd place)
- AP Rank: #8 (Mar 9th), seeded #11 on ESPN
- SRS: 23.44 (10th nationally), SOS: 12.91 (8th)
- ORtg: 117.3 (49th), DRtg: 101.7 (58th)
- Coach: Tom Izzo (31st year) — legendary March coach
- Notable wins: Kentucky, North Carolina, Illinois, Purdue, Indiana (x2)
- Lost to Duke, Michigan (x2), Nebraska, Minnesota, Wisconsin, UCLA (x2)

**North Dakota State (14 seed):**
- Record: 27-7 (14-2 Summit League, 1st place)
- Summit League regular season AND tournament champion
- SRS: 2.50 (131st), SOS: -5.79 (280th)
- ORtg: 116.1 (56th), DRtg: 100.1 (42nd)
- Losses: Oregon St, UC Davis, Arkansas St, UC Irvine, UTEP, Denver, St Thomas (MN)

Key comparison: The SRS gap is enormous — 23.44 vs 2.50. Michigan State's schedule was vastly tougher (SOS 8th vs 280th). While NDSU's raw efficiency numbers look decent (ORtg 56th, DRtg 42nd), those were compiled against a very weak schedule. Michigan State under Tom Izzo is historically excellent in March. The 3-14 upset rate is around 15%.
